name: Chewy Oatmeal Cookie
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cups Firmly packaged light brown sugar
3 cups Quaker(R) Quick Oats (not instant or old fashioned)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
2. Grease a baking sheet with Butter flavored Crisco.
3. In a large bowl, combine the Butter flavored Crisco, light brown sugar, egg, milk, and vanilla extract. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended.
4. In another bowl, mix together oats, flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon.
5. Add the dry mixture to the creamed mixture at a low speed just until blended.
6. Stir in raisins and nuts.
7. Drop rounded tablespoonfuls of dough 2 inches apart onto the prepared baking sheet.
8. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until lightly browned. Cool for 2 minutes on the baking sheet.
9. Remove to a kitchen counter. Yield: About 2 1/2 dozen cookies.
VARIATIONS:
HALF-DIPPED COOKIES - Omit raisins and nuts. Bake and cool. Microwave 1 cup Duncan-Hines Dutch Fudge Frosting for 20-25 seconds, or until smooth and thin. Dip the top half of each cookie in frosting. Lay on waxed paper until set.
FAVORITE CHIP COOKIES - Omit raisins and nuts. Add 1 cup baking chips to the batter. Bake and cool.
MAPLE WALNUT COOKIES - Omit raisins. Add 1-1/2 teaspoons maple flavoring to the batter. Bake and cool. Frost the top of the cooled cookies with a mixture of 1 container Duncan Hines Vanilla Layer Cake Frosting and 1 teaspoon maple flavoring. Garnish with walnut halves.
